name: Attractive Character Storyline Rotation — Broadcast Editorial Calendar

sections: DotCom Secrets Secret #6 — Attractive Character Storylines (the 6 basic storylines + their plot templates)
DotCom Secrets Secret #8 — Daily Seinfeld Sequence (each story ties to an offer; doubles as blog content)

trigger.primary: Recurring editorial planning layer over the daily broadcast engine. Solves 'what do I write each day' by rotating the 6 Attractive Character storylines across the broadcast schedule, each slot tied to a value-ladder offer.
trigger.trigger_type_ghl: Scheduled broadcast slot (one per day) drawing from the rotation; no new contact trigger — runs on the existing seinfeld-broadcast segment.

sequence.purpose: Show the upside of hardship; deep bonding.
sequence.subjectAngle: Open loop on a fall + comeback.
sequence.bodyFramework: 'I had everything... Then ___ happened. I had to figure my way out of ___. But it turned out to be a blessing in disguise because I went through ___, and I learned ___. Now I ___.'  TIE TO: core transformation offer.

sequence.purpose: Polarize; convert fair-weather fans into diehards.
sequence.bodyFramework: Call out 'talkers vs do-ers'; force the reader to choose a side and identify with your camp.  TIE TO: community / membership / ascension offer.

sequence.purpose: Transformation proof; works in any market.
sequence.bodyFramework: Simple transformation arc (often tellable with pictures).  TIE TO: the result-product that produced the after.

sequence.purpose: Make the answer feel finally available.
sequence.subjectAngle: 'Wait til you hear about this amazing new thing I just discovered!'
sequence.bodyFramework: Wide-eyed enthusiasm about a breakthrough you tried.  TIE TO: webinar / teleseminar / new offer (book: huge for selling webinars).

sequence.purpose: Curiosity draws readers up the Value Ladder.
sequence.subjectAngle: 'I've got a secret... if you want to find out, you need to ___.'
sequence.bodyFramework: Dangle the secret; the action to learn it is the CTA.  TIE TO: lead magnet / next funnel up the ladder.
Tell a customer's success as the story; let proof do the selling.  TIE TO: the proof-driven / high-ticket / application offer.

copyPersona.primary: Brunson Attractive Character storylines (DCS Secret #6), each wrapped in the Seinfeld Hook-Story-Offer shell (seinfeld-broadcast-email).
copyPersona.secondary: Edwards offer-tie-in discipline ('which means...' + a Reason People Buy on every slot).

copy_persona.primary: Brunson Attractive Character storylines (DCS Secret #6), each wrapped in the Seinfeld Hook-Story-Offer shell (seinfeld-broadcast-email).
supporting: Edwards offer-tie-in discipline ('which means...' + a Reason People Buy on every slot).

ghlBuild.buildType: Planning/scheduling layer over the daily broadcast engine (daily-seinfeld-sequence). Not a contact-triggered workflow.
implementation: Maintain a rotation in a planning sheet OR as 6 pre-built draft Email Campaigns in GHL, one per storyline slot.

Each storyline's CTA points to the matching value-ladder funnel URL (offer routing): Loss&Redemption->core; UsVsThem->membership; Before&After->result product; AmazingDiscovery->webinar; SecretTelling->lead magnet; Testimonial->application/high-ticket.
Optional: segment the seinfeld-broadcast Smart List by interest tag so the storyline's offer matches subscriber interest.
Schedule one slot per day; refresh stories monthly so the rotation stays fresh.
ghlBuild.notes: No new workflow trigger beyond Template 1's segment; this template governs WHAT is sent each day and WHERE the CTA points. Pairs with the broadcast Smart List from daily-seinfeld-sequence.

kpis: open & click rate by storyline type (find your highest-converting story types)
faithfulness: All six storylines, their plot templates, the 'talkers vs do-ers' line, and the offer-tie requirement are taken directly from DotCom Secrets Secret #6 and Secret #8. The weekly-slot scheduling and value-ladder CTA routing are the Skill-44 operationalization of those storylines.
